mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly higher when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in > 60% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in an impending slowdown.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 55% of observations during late-cycle expansions, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index surpasses 200, elevated inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate falls confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects strong economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which directly supports equity prices and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of observations despite rising GDPC1.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output and economic weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods where INDPRO is below 110, or the threshold signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward testing.
